item.title: Inner and outer fetters, Beings are fettered by them. I ask Gotama, Who can be freed from this fetter?" ( Blessed One ) One with…
item.description:
Inner and outer fetters,
Beings are fettered by them.
I ask Gotama,
Who can be freed from this fetter?"

( Blessed One )
One with wisdom, established in virtue,
Developing mind and wisdom,
Ardent and heedful,
That bhikkhu is freed from the fetter.
One who has abandoned
Greed, hatred, and ignorance,
The taint-free Arahant,
That one is freed from the fetter.
Where name-and-form
Is completely destroyed without remainder,
Where the impediment of form-perception is removed,
There the fetter is cut.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 7.6
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Possessing six qualities, bhikkhus, even if one hears the good Dhamma, one cannot enter the fixed course of rightness in wholesome states. What six? He…
item.description:
Possessing six qualities, bhikkhus, even if one hears the good Dhamma, one cannot enter the fixed course of rightness in wholesome states. What six? He is possessed of kamma-obstruction, defilement-obstruction, result-obstruction; he has no faith, no desire (to listen), and he has evil wisdom.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 6.86
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, Ānanda, what is the path, what is the way leading to the abandoning of the five lower fetters? Here, Ānanda, a bhikkhu, through the…
item.description:
And, Ānanda, what is the path, what is the way leading to the abandoning of the five lower fetters? Here, Ānanda, a bhikkhu, through the seclusion from all bases (upadhiviveka), through the abandoning of unwholesome states, through the complete calming down of all bodily coarseness, secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states, enters and abides in the first jhāna, which is accompanied by initial and sustained thought, with rapture and happiness born of seclusion.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 64 – Mahāmālukyasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Sāriputta, while a noble disciple attains and abides in the joy born of seclusion, at that time five things do not occur for him: Suffering…
item.description:
Sāriputta, while a noble disciple attains and abides in the joy born of seclusion, at that time five things do not occur for him: Suffering and grief connected with sensual pleasures do not occur for him at that time. Pleasure and joy connected with sensual pleasures do not occur for him at that time. Suffering and grief connected with the unwholesome do not occur for him at that time. Pleasure and joy connected with the unwholesome do not occur for him at that time. Suffering and grief connected with the wholesome do not occur for him at that time. Sāriputta, while a noble disciple attains and abides in the joy born of seclusion, at that time these five things do not occur for him.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 5.176
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: One who lives without fever, At death does not grieve. If the wise person sees the path, Among the grieving, does not grieve. A bhikkhu…
item.description:
One who lives without fever,
At death does not grieve.
If the wise person sees the path,
Among the grieving, does not grieve.

A bhikkhu who has cut off craving for existence,
With mind at peace,
The wandering in birth and death is ended,
That one is not reborn.

From: Udāna 4.9
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Venerable Mettagū: I ask the Blessed One, may the Blessed One answer me, The question I have asked — I think the Lord is wise,…
item.description:
Venerable Mettagū:
I ask the Blessed One, may the Blessed One answer me,
The question I have asked — I think the Lord is wise,
Self-developed. From what in this world does this suffering arise,
With so many kinds?

The Blessed One replied to Mettagū:
You ask me about the origin of suffering;
I will tell you as I have known:
From a basis, suffering arises, with many kinds,
Various in the world.

Those who, because of ignorance, create a basis,
That fool repeatedly encounters suffering.
Therefore, a wise person should not create a basis,
Seeing the origin of birth and suffering.

From: Sutta Nipāta 5.5
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: The unreal they take for real, The real they take for unreal. They never reach the real, By their wrong thoughts and wrong conduct. Having…
item.description:
The unreal they take for real,
The real they take for unreal.
They never reach the real,
By their wrong thoughts and wrong conduct.

Having known the real as real,
And the unreal as unreal,
They reach the real,
By their right thoughts and right conduct.

From: Dhammapada 1-20
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: When going to an assembly or a gathering, Or when alone with a single person, Do not speak falsehood, nor cause others to speak it,…
item.description:
When going to an assembly or a gathering,
Or when alone with a single person,
Do not speak falsehood, nor cause others to speak it,
Nor approve of false speech. Abandon all that is untrue.

Do not live a life of drinking intoxicants.
A lay follower who has accepted this Dhamma
Should not cause others to drink nor approve of drinking.
Knowing that drinking leads to madness.

Only fools, when drunk, do evil deeds
And cause other heedless people to live heedlessly.
Avoid this ground of non-merit,
Which deludes and delights the fool.

From: Sutta Nipāta 2.14
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Friends, what is the cessation of suffering? It is the complete fading away and cessation of that craving, its abandonment, relinquishment, liberation, and non-attachment. Friends,…
item.description:
Friends, what is the cessation of suffering? It is the complete fading away and cessation of that craving, its abandonment, relinquishment, liberation, and non-attachment. Friends, what is the path leading to the cessation of suffering? It is just this Noble Eightfold Path leading to the cessation of suffering, namely: Right View, Right Intention, Right Speech, Right Action, Right Livelihood, Right Effort, Right Mindfulness, Right Concentration.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 9 – Sammādiṭṭhisutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Brahmin, for what reason, for what condition, given that Rājagaha exists, given that the path to Rājagaha exists, given that you exist as a guide,…
item.description:
Brahmin, for what reason, for what condition, given that Rājagaha exists, given that the path to Rājagaha exists, given that you exist as a guide, though you instruct and teach thus, one person takes the wrong path and goes westward, while another person can reach Rājagaha safely?" — "Master Gotama, what can I do? I am only a guide, Master Gotama." — "So too, brahmin, Nibbāna exists, the path to Nibbāna exists, and I exist as a guide. But my disciples, instructed and taught thus by me, some attain the ultimate goal of Nibbāna, some do not attain it. Here, brahmin, what can I do? The Tathāgata is only a guide.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 107 – Gaṇakamoggallānasutta
📖 Read this sutta
